outcomes.metric: editorial work via the AI pipeline
epic delivery via SDD, with reviewable specs + example code

video.caption: Inside the high-load sports platform & the AI content service.

description: Backend and frontend (Twig) development — creating new services, fixing bugs, and adding features. I refactored legacy code, proposed improvement concepts, sped it up, and made it more flexible and easier to maintain.
key_achievements: Generative AI: built and maintained an AI content-generation service on OpenAI and Claude — text generation via the Batch API, news embeddings, content checks via the Moderation API, and automatic match video-predictions. Added an AI content-review step that flags rude or low-quality text before publishing.

Memory & stability: fixed a memory leak in a back-office consumer that ran on every admin-panel save — cutting its peak memory from a 2 GB overflow to ~16 MB — and made it stateless by resetting from config on each run, so a job never executes against a previous message's configuration.

Observability & incident resolution: diagnosed and resolved production errors via Sentry; built Grafana dashboards for cache hit-ratio and queue analysis; integrated Prometheus metrics; introduced compression in Memcached.

Performance & caching: optimized cache strategies and TTL (Redis / Memcached), resolved cache-invalidation issues and race conditions, and optimized SQL / GraphQL queries and the MongoDB config in a real-time system.
Asynchronous queues: Symfony Messenger + Redis — eliminated queue congestion and transport errors, deduplicated parser messages, and implemented adaptive queues.
Architecture refactoring: decomposed monolithic WordPress plugins into separate services (cookie, widget-logic, sportsteam-widget, football-standings, azscore).
Facebook Graph API: auto-posting of news to Facebook pages and groups, geo-parameterization of posts (country_code), and publication-error diagnostics.
SEO & i18n: Hreflang, meta-tags, canonical URLs, multilingual support, and locale handling across preprod / prod environments on Kubernetes.
Real-time data: WordPress plugins fed from our data over WebSockets via Centrifugo.
responsibility: Backend and full-stack development, production-incident resolution, performance and cache optimization, observability, asynchronous queues, and third-party API integration
